name: AML/CTF & Financial-Sanctions Legal Corpus — UN · AU · UK · US · EU (v1)
tagline: A neutral, provenance-first record of anti-money-laundering / counter-terrorism-financing and financial-sanctions law across four cross-referenced jurisdictions (Australia, the United Kingdom, the United States, the European Union), rooted in the UN treaty and Security Council backbone, with the FATF standard as the connective spine. Every text carries its official source, retrieval date, citation, jurisdiction, legal status, language, an authoritative-status flag, and a SHA-256 hash; every change is a new dated version. A reference record of the law — not legal or compliance advice.

card.pretty_name: AML/CTF & Financial-Sanctions Legal Corpus (UN · Australia · UK · US · EU, v1)
card.description: A neutral, **provenance-first**, machine-readable record of the law of anti-money-laundering / counter-terrorism-financing (AML/CTF) and financial sanctions across four cross-referenced jurisdictions — Australia, the United Kingdom, the United States and the European Union — rooted in the UN treaty and Security Council backbone, with the FATF 40 Recommendations as the connective spine. Every record carries its official source, retrieval date, citation, jurisdiction, legal status, language, an authoritative-status flag, and a SHA-256 content hash; every change is a new dated version.
card.license_terms_examples: Commonwealth of Australia legislation under CC BY 4.0; UK legislation Crown copyright under OGL v3.0; UN official documents free to reproduce with attribution; FATF/OECD material under copyright, restricted
card.author: Dan Cheah
card.concepts_note: curated (human-reviewed) for the main instruments, keyword-derived elsewhere; unofficial
